concepts.title: Group 15: Properties of Nitrogen
concepts.description: Nitrogen is a nonmetal, colorless, odorless, and tasteless gas. It is a major constituent of the atmosphere.

concepts.title: Group 17: Halogens
concepts.description: Halogens are highly reactive nonmetals, which readily accept electrons to form halides.

concepts.title: Group 18: Noble Gases
concepts.description: Noble gases are chemically inert and have full outer energy levels, which makes them unreactive.

concepts.title: p-Block Elements: Order of Electron Gain Enthalpy
concepts.description: The order of electron gain enthalpy for p-block elements is: F > Cl > Br > I > At, which can be remembered by the phrase 'Female Cats Bring Infections Always Today'.

concepts.title: Group 15: Nitrogen Compounds
concepts.description: Students often confuse between ammonia (NH3) and hydrazoic acid (HN3). The key difference is the position of the nitrogen atom in ammonia and the presence of a triple bond in hydrazoic acid.

concepts.title: Group 16: Oxygen Family
concepts.description: The members of the oxygen family are oxygen (O), sulfur (S), selenium (Se), tellurium (Te), and polonium (Po).

concepts.title: Group 17: Halogen Properties
concepts.description: Halogens are highly reactive and readily form compounds with metals and nonmetals. They have high electron affinity and are good oxidizing agents.

concepts.title: Group 18: Noble Gases Uses
concepts.description: Noble gases have various uses, including neon signs, argon-filled light bulbs, and krypton lasers.
